M-042.NAME: mtproto-assignment-registry
M-042.owns: PostgreSQL-backed MTProto proxy assignment records, uniqueness constraints, lifecycle status, and restore-safe metadata.
M-042.purpose: Store each user's personal Telegram MTProto proxy assignment inside KrotPN's primary database instead of KPprotoN DETS.

exports.model-MTProtoAssignment: User-linked assignment table with SNI, credential mode, status, timestamps, and rotation metadata.
exports.fn-get-user-assignment: Return the current active or latest assignment for one user.
exports.fn-save-assignment: Create or update an idempotent user assignment.
exports.fn-list-assignments: Admin-safe paginated lookup without exposing raw secrets by default.

data-contract.field-1: user_id has a uniqueness guard for one canonical active personal proxy per user.
data-contract.field-2: sni is unique and remains stable across idempotent reissue unless rotation is explicit.
data-contract.field-3: secret material is derived on demand or encrypted if persisted; raw proxy links are not logged.

constraints.constraint-1: Backup/restore must preserve assignment linkage with users and migration state.
constraints.constraint-2: Schema changes require Alembic coverage and SQLModel compatibility.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Phase-29 target pytest passed for assignment registry, SNI uniqueness, redacted listing, and migration metadata.

M-042.hotspot: H-028: MTProto assignment backup/restore must preserve SNI, credential mode, and user linkage without storing unnecessary raw secrets
